name: aicsimageio (Bio-Image I/O with Lazy Loading)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); Allen Institute for Cell Science

auth.notes: Local library; no auth required; S3/GCS support via fsspec

performance.notes: Dask lazy loading for large microscopy files; scales to TB via chunked reading

agent_readiness.notes: Unified bio-image reading with lazy Dask loading. from aicsimageio import AICSImage. img = AICSImage('image.tif'). img = AICSImage('s3://bucket/image.zarr'). img.dims → Dimensions(TCZYX). img.shape → (T, C, Z, Y, X). img.get_image_dask_data('ZYX', C=1) → dask array. img.get_image_data('ZYX', T=0, C=0) → numpy array. img.xarray_dask_data → xarray DataArray with dimension labels. Formats: OME-TIFF, OME-Zarr, CZI (Zeiss), LIF (Leica), ND2 (Nikon), TIFF, PNG, JPEG. Metadata: img.channel_names, img.physical_pixel_sizes. Writer: from aicsimageio.writers import OmeTiffWriter. OmeTiffWriter.save(data, 'out.ome.tif', channel_names=['DAPI']).

gotchas: Format-specific readers have separate install: pip install aicsimageio[czi] for CZI
Dask lazy array requires .compute() or chunk iteration — not loaded by default
OME-Zarr remote reading requires zarr and fsspec with appropriate storage backend
Physical pixel size metadata often missing or wrong in non-OME formats
best_when: Reading large multi-dimensional microscopy images (CZI, LIF, ND2, OME-TIFF)
Lazy loading of TB-scale image stacks without memory errors
Cloud-based image reading from S3/GCS via OME-Zarr
Standardizing TCZYX dimension order across different microscope brands
avoid_when: Simple 2D image reading (use imageio or Pillow)
When all data must be in memory (use tifffile directly)
summary: aicsimageio reads bio-images lazily with Dask. AICSImage('file.czi'). img.dims → TCZYX. get_image_dask_data('ZYX', C=1). xarray_dask_data. OME-TIFF, OME-Zarr, CZI, LIF, ND2, TIFF. S3/GCS via fsspec. OmeTiffWriter. BSD-3 by Allen Institute. Best for large multi-dimensional microscopy with lazy loading. Format-specific readers installed separately. .compute() needed for numpy arrays. Physical pixel size metadata unreliable for non-OME formats.
